Barrel Sauna UK

Floating Sauna Proposal  ·  Prepared for Sanju
01 · Introduction

A floating sauna, built for the long game

You came to us with a clear idea: a commercial floating sauna on the water, built right the first time, ready to anchor a business you can take nationwide.

This proposal introduces the concept we are picturing for your pilot, shows you the work we already have running as live commercial businesses, and sets out an estimated cost at this early stage. It also explains exactly what the design commitment secures, and what happens next.

What follows is an initial concept and an estimate. The bespoke design, drawn around your site and your guests, comes once you are on board.

Having everything right on the first go is non-negotiable for us.

Your words on the call, and the principle this whole proposal is built around.

02 · About Barrel Sauna UK

A Welsh family business, handmade to order

We are a family-run manufacturer based in Wales, building bespoke saunas and wellness structures from scratch for homes and commercial venues across the UK and Ireland.

Every unit we make is built to order in our own workshop. Nothing is flat-packed, nothing is imported and rebadged. We design and fabricate the whole thing ourselves, which is what lets us tailor each build to the client rather than the other way round.

That matters most on a commercial project. When a build has to run as a live business, pass inspection, and stand up to daily use, being the people who actually made it is the difference between a supplier and a partner. We are UK-based, we answer the phone, and when something needs attention we make the trip.

03 · Why clients choose us

Made in Wales, and built to last

The reasons clients pick us over a cheaper import are the same reasons a commercial build stays standing years after a flat-pack would have needed replacing.

Manufactured in WalesDesigned and fabricated in our own workshop, start to finish. We control the build, so we can tailor it to you.
Bespoke, built to orderNo two builds are the same. Layout, glazing, heating and finish are all specified around your site and your guests.
UK-based aftercareDevon, Cornwall, Scotland, Ireland. Anything within warranty and within reason, we come back out and put it right.
Sustainability

Timber that gives back

Our builds are made from premium thermowood, heat-treated for durability and stability without chemical preservatives. It is a material chosen to last decades outdoors, which is the most sustainable thing a structure can do: not need replacing.

We plant ten trees for every one we use, so the material at the heart of every build is renewed many times over rather than simply spent. A commitment made consistently, on every project we take on.

Every sauna supports a greener future
We plant 10 trees for every one we use

05 · Why we can build this

A floating platform is a natural next step for us

A floating sauna is a new concept for us, and we are being straight with you about that. What is not new is the hard part: fabricating the structure.

We already manufacture our own trailer chassis, steel frames and supporting structures in-house. Designing a floating platform draws on exactly that experience: load, balance, a steel base built to carry a timber structure safely. It is an extension of what we do every week, not a leap into the unknown.

That in-house fabrication is also why we can stand behind a bespoke commercial build. We are not assembling someone else's parts; we are engineering the whole thing, which means when your project needs something specific, we can make it.

06 · Initial concept

How we are thinking about the layout

A glass-fronted, wood-fired straight-edge sauna on a floating pontoon, designed around privacy, compliance, and low-maintenance running.

The concept below carries the essentials you set out on the call: a wood-burning stove with the log feed running through the changing room, a changing room at each end kept private, a covered front deck, and warm, low lighting behind the backrests and under the seats only, so the space stays private for guests in swimwear.

This is the arrangement from your brief. If you have a better idea for the layout, the design stage is where we shape it with you until it is right for your site.

Concept illustration only. The visuals shown are to communicate the initial idea. The final design, layout and specification will be developed during the paid design stage.

07 · Estimated cost

An estimate to plan around

This is our estimated build cost at this stage, not a fixed quotation. Anything project-specific is worked through properly once the design stage begins.

Estimated build cost
£90,000 to £120,000

An indicative range for a commercial floating sauna of this scale. The final figure is confirmed at the design stage, once the layout, specification and site are locked. Delivery and certification are separate and shown below.

  • Delivery to siteSeparate · varies by mooring location
  • CertificationSeparate · arranged per site

Delivery and certification vary by where the unit is moored and how it is brought to site, so they are kept as separate line items rather than folded into the headline figure. We work these through with you once the design is underway and the logistics are known.

08 · The design commitment

What the £500 sets in motion

On a build like this, the design commitment is not a queue slot. It is what puts the real design and planning work into motion, and it comes off your final invoice if you go ahead.

Before we can give you a firm figure and a delivery plan, there is real work to do: developing your bespoke design, checking certification, pricing delivery, and working out the logistics of building and moving a unit this size. That work takes time and cost, so the commitment comes before it, not after.

In James's words on the call: he is not going to sit and work out exactly what it costs and how to deliver it until he knows you are serious. The £500 is the point at which we move from concept into developing your project properly.

The next step

£500 design commitment

Sets your bespoke design and planning in motion, and is deducted from your final invoice if you proceed.

  • ✓
    Your bespoke concept designConceptual drawings for your build, drawn around your site, delivered within a week of your commitment.
  • ✓
    A design refinement stageWe work the concept through with you until it is right for your site. A considered refinement stage, not an open-ended process.
  • ✓
    Costing and delivery planningCertification checks, delivery pricing, and the logistics of building and moving a unit this size, worked out properly.
What happens next

From commitment to delivery

  • Design commitment and conceptOnce your £500 is in, your concept drawings land within a week. We then refine the design with you until it is right.
  • Sign-off and orderWhen the design is right and you are ready, your build goes into the queue and the balance terms are confirmed.
  • Build and lead timeCurrent build lead time is around seven to eight weeks. Allowing for scheduling and delivery, plan for roughly three months from order to delivery, a little more for the pontoon.
  • Install and handoverOur team installs, walks every element with you on site, and signs off, which activates your warranty.

An initial concept, and a starting point

Take this through with your partner. When you are ready to commit, we will have your bespoke concept underway within the week.
